mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative skew when both proxies decline concurrently, or the joint signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising consumer price inflation erodes real disposable income and suppresses consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as margin compression accelerates.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when inflation and credit spreads widen simultaneously, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, which directly supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls fall below 100,000 for two consecutive months, it confirms a labor market cooling cycle that historically precedes equity drawdowns, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls below 100000 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with contracting industrial output signal a liquidity squeeze on capital-intensive sectors, which reduces forward earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative skew when both proxies move in the specified direction, or the joint signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for interest rate volatility.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust employment growth, rising consumer credit balances amplify household spending power, which supports retail and consumer discretionary earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain negative when credit and employment data improve simultaneously, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward testing.
